implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Clean, minimal implementation that meets both acceptance criteria. Footer with dynamic year and 'BrewBoard' text added in 4 lines. Only minor quibble: <footer> is nested inside <main> rather than as a sibling, which is semantically imprecise but functionally correct for this simple app.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met: footer appears at the bottom of the page content and contains copyright text with current year and 'BrewBoard'. Spec says 'bottom of every page' but the technical approach itself scopes to page.tsx, which is the only page in this seed app. Minor deduction for the footer being inside <main> rather than a page-level element — it's at the bottom of content but not architecturally at the bottom of the page.

correctness.notes: new Date().getFullYear() is correct JSX for dynamic year rendering. Tailwind classes are valid. The <footer> semantic element inside <main> is slightly unusual (landmark inside landmark) but renders correctly and won't cause runtime errors.

minimality.notes: 4 insertions, 1 file changed. No tests, no docs, no refactoring — exactly what the spec's out-of-scope section demands. Perfectly scoped.

code_quality.notes: Readable one-liner with sensible Tailwind classes. text-stone-500 fits the BrewBoard stone palette. mt-12 provides appropriate spacing. Consistent with the surrounding JSX style.

risk.notes: No UX or accessibility regressions. <footer> is a landmark element which is good for screen readers, though nesting it inside <main> means assistive tech may not treat it as the page-level contentinfo landmark. No maintainability concerns.
